repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Collection contains business documents, correspondence, engineering plans and schematics, audio and video recordings, photographs, instruments and instrument components, and other personal papers documenting his work as an engineer and electronic music pioneer.
did.physdesc: Audio Recordings, Computer Media, Correspondence, Financial Records, Legal Documents, Manuscripts, Memorabilia, Newspapers, Photographs, Printed Materials, Publications, Research Materials

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Robert Moog, founder of Moog Music, was an American engineer and pioneer of electronic music, best known as the inventor of the Moog synthesizer.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Collection contains business documents, correspondence, engineering plans and schematics, audio and video recordings, photographs, instruments and instrument components, and other personal papers documenting his work as an engineer and electronic music pioneer.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use:
userestrict.p: Software and digital files stored on obsolete formats are closed to researchers pending digital processing.

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use:
userestrict.p: Mold present in all open-reel tapes and videotapes except the following: tr-16422-16426, 16428-16430, 16436-16437, 16439, 16445, 16465-16466, 16468-16470, 16473-16477, 16480, 16486, V-10451-10453, and V-10871. Individuals with weak immune systems may need to take extra precautions.

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use:
userestrict.p: Phonograph records in boxes 19, 71, and 109 have been remediated for mold. Consider using nitrile gloves when directly handling.

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use:
userestrict.p: Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on our Reproductions & Permissions page.

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use:
userestrict.p: Audiovisual media that has been digitized: TR-16146, TR-16347, TR-16377, TR-16509.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Robert Moog papers, #8629. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

p.ref: Series IV. Schematics, Circuit Notes, and Layouts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes college notebooks, personal finance, and articles/notes related to personal interests and activities.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically, except where noted otherwise.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains bills, check stubs, and bank statements.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to box 57 restricted to the permission of the curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains bills, bank statements, taxes, and life insurance/health information.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to box 24 restricted to the permission of the curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notebooks, assignments, and readings from Moog's years at Cornell. Includes his 1965 thesis as well as his BA diploma from Queen's College.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Lecture notes, notebooks, and assignments arranged by course, as per original order.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to folders 36 and 40-41 restricted to the permission of the curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains: articles and notes related to various interests and activities of Moog's including: political organizing with the ACLU; pamphlets from Florence Moog's memorial as well as a selection of her writings; documents pertaining to Moog genealogy; and travel ephemera.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged by subject.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains notebooks and news clippings, arranged by subject as per original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains notebooks and news clippings, arranged by subject as per original order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Alphabetical files (boxes 60-61) includes product inquiries, fan mail, catalog requests, invitations, greeting cards, and personal correspondence. Includes handwritten and typewritten letters, faxes, printed emails, photocopies, and carbon copies.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by last name of correspondent (or corporate name where applicable).

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Correspondence was removed from envelopes by processing staff. Where letters do not include full dates and/or names, materials were kept in original envelopes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes address lists and correspondence with individuals whose last names could not be determined.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence between Florence Moog and various members of the Moog family (including Robert) as well as correspondence between Florence and friends.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence between Florence Moog and various friends and family members.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence between Moog and various family members, primarily Shirleigh Moog.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged by year.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes financial records, meeting notes and minutes, phone logs, production notes and planning, press releases, company newsletters, product manuals and advertisements for companies Moog operated or was employed by.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: In order to preserve the context of materials in this series, correspondence related to business-operations and product development was not moved to the alphabetized Correspondence series.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains bills, petty cash receipts, and expenditures (1976-1979, 1982-1989).

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to box 51 restricted to the permission of the curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains R.A. Moog Co., Moog muSonics, and Moog Music/Norlin business documents, including meeting minutes, press releases, sales reports, memos, phone logs, and documentation of product planning, engineering, and production.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains Big Briar, Crumar, Synton, and Kurzweil business documents, including meeting minutes, press releases, sales reports, memos, phone logs, and documentation of product planning, engineering, and production. Also contains 1983-1984 business correspondence.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: 1983-1984 correspondence was originally organized alphabetically in an accordion file labeled "Business Correspondence." Correspondence was removed from the file , but original alphabetical order has been preserved.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains Big Briar, Kurzweil, and Van Koevering business documents, including meeting minutes, press releases, sales reports, memos, phone logs, and documentation of product planning, engineering, and production.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains Big Briar, Van Koevering, and Moog Music business documents, including meeting minutes, press releases, sales reports, memos, phone logs, and documentation of product planning, engineering, and production. Also contains documentation of trademark disputes regarding the Moog company name.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains invoices and packing slips (1968-1999) and bills/receipts (1990-2003). Invoices for 1983-1984 are filed in the "Business Correspondence" section of box 48.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains owner's manuals, service manuals, and rough drafts of manuals.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Additional drafts of manuals for certain products are present in Series IV.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuals (1985-2000, folders 1-15); catalogs, brochures, advertisements, price lists, and newsletters (1957-2011).

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Additional drafts of manuals for certain products are present in Series IV.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poster-sized advertisement for R.A. Moog and Magnetic Recording Systems, Inc (MRS)

did.unittitle: Wendy Carlos and Keith Emerson Moog advertisement paste ups
Big Briar 100/300 Series Controllers advertisement paste up

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains financial records, legal correspondence, stock transfers, and other records documenting the sale of Moog to Waytena and subsequently to Norlin. Also contains undated notes and miscellaneous tax documents dated 1980-1999.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to box 25 restricted to the permission of the curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains check stubs and bank statements (1981-1987) and taxes (1976-1982).

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to box 23 restricted to the permission of the curator.

did.unittitle: Series IV. Schematics, Circuit Notes, and Layouts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes hand drawn and printed schematics, notes, PCB and panel layouts for circuits developed primarily in the 1960s-1970s. Includes custom designs and modifications for individuals including Wendy Carlos, Keith Emerson, Jan Hammer, Eddie Harris, John Eaton, and Max Brand. Includes associated correspondence with collaborators and customers where applicable.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by project/product name, last name (where project was commissioned or collaborated on), or manufacturer (where files are neither Moog nor Big Briar, i.e.. Maestro, Hammond, Fairlight, etc.). Within each subseries files are arranged chronologically.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Original folders were discarded due to their strong musty odor, though folder titles were transcribed verbatim from Moog's original files.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Items in this series were originally found folded in letter sized folders. Folded items have been removed from their original folders, flattened, and stored in separate oversize folders. In such instances, folders are listed as "Title 1 of 2," "Title 2 of 2," etc. to indicate their original connection.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: In some cases loose files were consolidated into project files.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Drafts of product manuals were left in original folders and have not been moved to the manuals series.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes flyers, invitations, correspondence, and travel arrangements for various conferences, events, and speaking engagements. Includes printed lectures by Moog where applicable. Events include academic conferences, computer/electronic/experimental music conferences, trade shows, university commencements , workshops, and television appearances.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes conference preprints, ephemera, and advertisements, as well as copies of paper reviews by Moog (with their corresponding papers).

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by author. Exceptions include subject-based files which were kept intact (i.e.. "Vocoder files" and "Delta modulation").

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes articles written by Moog, as well as a complete run of the Moog-published Electronic Music Review (1967-1968). Includes 2 reels of Electronic Music Review microfilm. Issues of Contemporary Keyboard Magazine featuring columns by Moog are located in box 42. Speeches delivered by Moog are filed in their corresponding event file in Series V.

did.unittitle: Strobe Tuner Settings for the Historical Scales

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains newspaper and magazine clippings of: Moog advertisements, articles about Moog, interviews with/quotes from Moog, and photographs of Moog instruments, collected by both Robert Moog and the Bob Moog Foundation. Also includes articles collected by Moog about electronic music and performers associated with the company, including Wendy Carlos, Lydia Kavina, Clara Rockmore, and Keith Emerson.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically by decade.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains Electronotes (1973-1982); Dr. Dobb's Journal (2003); Sound on Sound (2004); Music Perception (1984-1985); IEEE Spectrum (2003); Keyboard Magazine (2003-2004); Journal of the Acoustical Society of America (1974).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains The New Yorker (1990-1993); The Journal of Music Theory (1963-1969); Wired (2001); Sound on Sound (2002-2003); Attaché (1998); Electronic Musician (2002-2004); Keyboard (2003-2005); Keyboard Magazine (2001); miscellaneous magazines (1995-2005).

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Prints and negatives arranged alphabetically within subject subseries. Slides arranged separately.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Photographs and slides were partially processed by the Bob Moog Foundation. Folder titles in this series were devised largely by the Foundation, with some exceptions titled by Moog. A large number of photographs were unlabeled, and as such were given titles by processing staff.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Many of the items in this series were marked with a cataloging number such as "PHO 326" by the Bob Moog Foundation. These cataloging numbers were preserved where possible to reflect the previous custodian's original order.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on our Reproductions & Permissions page.
